notes: Agent self-reports task progress via an in-band hidden marker (irrlicht-eta) driven by a persistent instruction-file rule. For gemini-cli the rule lives in the GLOBAL ~/.gemini/GEMINI.md (loaded for every project; HOME is not isolated in the driver, so it reaches the fresh per-run cwd). gemini does NOT strip HTML comments at injection (processImports leaves comments + code-block content verbatim; only @import inside backticks is skipped), so the fenced marker example reaches the model intact. The geminicli parser feeds the FULL untruncated assistant text to the shared tailer.ScanTaskEstimate (parser.go:167), surfacing TaskEstimate {total_rounds, completed_rounds, updated_at} on SessionMetrics, and the shared ForecastTaskCompletion projects task_completion_eta at the measured rate (elapsed/completed). The PHASE DSL asserts only the user-observable lifecycle arc — a multi-turn ready->working->ready walk to a ready finish; the estimate-content invariants (completed_rounds present + non-decreasing + reaching total_rounds; updated_at advancing; ETA present once >=1 round reported and burning down; fields present only while working / absent at ready) live in the scenario acceptance_criteria + recipe verify[]. Emission is model-discretion (soft) and gemini emission is unverified — record settles it. observations.model pinned to gemini-3.5-flash (the live API-key-tier default, confirmed by the 2026-06-12 recording; the OAuth-tier default was gemini-3-flash-preview).

text: Session appears in ready (first phase anchored UNPINNED to start so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal birth)
First prompt (plan 5 phases, emit 0/5 then advance to 1/5) drives ready -> working; the first marker is emitted and task_estimate.total_rounds=5 appears on metrics
Turn 1 completes back to ready; completed_rounds=1 (the first reported round) — task_completion_eta now projectable
Phase-2 prompt drives ready -> working; marker advances toward 2/5 (completed_rounds non-decreasing, updated_at advances)
Turn 2 completes back to ready; ETA after this marker <= ETA after turn 1 (burns down)
Phase-3 prompt drives ready -> working; marker advances toward 3/5
Phase-4 prompt drives ready -> working; marker advances toward 4/5
Final phase-5 prompt drives ready -> working; marker reaches 5/5 (completed_rounds == total_rounds by the final working turn)
Final turn completes working -> ready; final session state is ready; the >=30s trailing idle captures the post-turn settle, after which the client suppresses the ETA chip (present only while working)
